qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Command injection via Runtime.exec() in IAST smoke test endpoint permits arbitrary code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a command injection vulnerability in the IAST smoke test application's /cmdi/runtime endpoint, which is a different vulnerability class (command_injection vs deserialization_rce), different root cause (Runtime.exec with unvalidated input vs unsafe RMI deserialization without serialization filters), and different component (smoke test application vs production RMI instrumentation) than the advisory-described vulnerability.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes unsafe deserialization in dd-trace-java's RMI instrumentation (specifically, a custom RMI endpoint that deserializes incoming data without serialization filters on JDK 16 or earlier). The cmdi-runtime-exec finding is a command injection in the IAST smoke test application, which is a different vulnerability class and component. This finding does not match the advisory's root cause: missing serialization filters in the RMI instrumentation layer of dd-trace-java.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-579q-h82j-r5v2 describes CWE-502 (Deserialization of Untrusted Data) in dd-trace-java's RMI instrumentation
Advisory specifies the vulnerable component: RMI instrumentation that registered a custom endpoint deserializing incoming data without serialization filters
Advisory requires three conditions: (1) dd-trace-java attached as Java agent on JDK 16 or earlier, (2) JMX/RMI port configured and network-reachable, (3) gadget-chain-compatible library present

cmdi-runtime-exec finding identifies command injection in IAST smoke test application (/cmdi/runtime endpoint using Runtime.exec), which is a different vulnerability class (CWE-78 vs CWE-502) and component (test application vs production RMI instrumentation)
The finding does not reference the specific RMI instrumentation code, serialization filter bypass, or JMX/RMI port configuration described in the advisory
